reports.title: Trump's Iran Ceasefire: Victory or Surrender?
reports.subtitle: The strongman who threatened to destroy Iran just settled for a two-week pause.

reports.title: Social Media's Tobacco Moment
reports.subtitle: A jury just declared Instagram and YouTube liable for addiction — and tech's legal immunity era might be over.

reports.title: Microsoft's Encryption Software Squeeze
reports.subtitle: Locking out VeraCrypt developers reveals how platform gatekeepers can kill security tools they don't control.

reports.title: The Government Parent Arrives
reports.subtitle: Greece bans social media for under-15s, joining a global shift where governments override parental authority over children's digital lives.

reports.title: Meta's AI Mulligan
reports.subtitle: A 'ground-up overhaul' with Muse Spark reveals how far behind Meta fell in the AI race — and how desperate they are to catch up.

reports.title: Iran's 10-Point Plan and the Coerced Ceasefire
reports.subtitle: A hostage exchange disguised as diplomacy — both sides are trading things they can't afford to lose.

reports.title: Sánchez's Bucket and What It Costs to Say No Inside NATO
reports.subtitle: Spain's PM said the obvious thing — you don't get credit for putting out a fire you started. The interesting question is why he's the only one.

reports.title: China and Russia Kill the Hormuz Lifeline
reports.subtitle: Beijing and Moscow didn't veto a resolution protecting shipping — they vetoed the idea that the UN Security Council has any authority over a war they're quietly backing.

reports.title: OpenAI's Robin Hood Pitch
reports.subtitle: The company racing hardest to automate the global workforce has published a 13-page plan to compensate everyone it displaces — which is either the most responsible move in AI history or the most successful rebranding.

reports.title: The $166 Billion Tax Nobody Gets Back
reports.subtitle: The Supreme Court ruled Trump's tariffs illegal. American businesses and families paid $166 billion. One year later, the government has not issued a single refund — and is imposing new tariffs to replace the overturned ones.

reports.title: India Revokes the Right to Know Who You Are
reports.subtitle: A 2014 Supreme Court ruling said transgender Indians had a constitutional right to self-identify their gender. The 2026 amendment says the state, through a medical board, will decide instead.

reports.title: The Strike That Reveals Who Owns Your Steak
reports.subtitle: JBS workers ended the first major US meatpacking strike since 1985 without a new contract — returning to work into a supply chain where their employer is recording record profits while beef is unaffordable for the people who process it.

reports.title: The End of Congressional Subpoenas
reports.subtitle: The Supreme Court cleared the way for Steve Bannon's contempt conviction to be dismissed — not on a legal principle, but on a presidential request. Congress's power to compel testimony from executive allies has now been tested and found unenforceable.
